Credit Guide

ASIC RG 206 compliant credit guide providing essential information about our commercial finance brokerage services, licensing, fees, and your rights under the National Consumer Credit Protection Act 2009.

Last Updated: 15 August 2025 Next Review: 15 August 2026 ASIC Compliant: RG 206

Important Information

This Credit Guide is provided to you under section 126 of the National Consumer Credit Protection Act 2009. It contains important information about our credit services, including our Australian Credit Licence details, the services we provide, how we are remunerated, and your rights and obligations.

Please read this document carefully before engaging our services.

Quick Navigation

Licensee Information Credit Representative Services Provided Fees & Commission Conflicts of Interest Complaints Process Privacy Information Contact Details

Australian Credit Licensee Information

Credit Licensee

Entity Name: Edgeview Finance Business Solutions Pty Ltd

Australian Business Number (ABN): XX XXX XXX XXX

Australian Credit Licence (ACL): ACL-XXXXXX

ASIC Registration: Australian Credit Licensee

Licence Authorisation

Our Australian Credit Licence authorises us to engage in credit activities including:

  • Providing credit assistance by suggesting or assisting with credit contract applications
  • Suggesting or assisting with credit contract changes or refinancing
  • Suggesting credit providers to customers
  • Assisting customers to understand credit contract features and risks

Address:

Australia

Business Hours: Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM AEST

ASIC Verification

You can verify our licence details on the ASIC Professional Registers at https://connectonline.asic.gov.au using our ACL number ACL-XXXXXX.

Credit Representative Information

Authorised Credit Representative

[Credit Representative Name] operates as an authorised credit representative of Edgeview Finance Business Solutions Pty Ltd under our Australian Credit Licence.

Representative Details

Name: [Credit Representative Name]

Credit Representative Number: [CRN Number]

Qualifications: Certificate IV in Finance and Mortgage Broking

Professional Memberships: Finance Brokers Association of Australia (FBAA)

Authorisation Scope

Our credit representative is authorised to:

  • Provide credit assistance for commercial finance products
  • Assist with loan applications and documentation
  • Liaise with lenders on behalf of clients
  • Provide ongoing account management and support
  • Conduct responsible lending assessments

Professional Standards

Our credit representative maintains professional standards through:

  • Annual continuing professional development (CPD) training
  • Adherence to the Code of Conduct for Credit Services
  • Regular compliance monitoring and audits
  • Professional indemnity insurance coverage
  • Membership of industry professional bodies

Credit Services We Provide

Commercial Finance Brokerage Services

Under our Australian Credit Licence, we provide credit assistance services specifically focused on commercial finance solutions for Australian businesses.

Equipment Finance

  • Commercial vehicle and machinery finance
  • Construction and trade equipment funding
  • Technology and IT equipment finance
  • Chattel mortgages and hire purchase agreements
  • Operating and finance lease arrangements

Loan Range: $20,000 to $2,000,000+

Commercial Property Finance

  • Business premises purchase and refinancing
  • Commercial property investment loans
  • Development and construction finance
  • Strata title and industrial property finance
  • Commercial mortgage solutions

Loan Range: $100,000 to $10,000,000+

Working Capital Solutions

  • Business line of credit facilities
  • Overdraft and cash flow funding
  • Invoice factoring and discounting
  • Trade finance and debtor finance
  • Seasonal and project-specific funding

Facility Range: $50,000 to $5,000,000+

Personal Lending (Business-Related)

  • Self-employed home loans
  • Investment property finance for business owners
  • Low-doc and bank statement lending
  • Asset-based lending solutions
  • Private lending arrangements

Loan Range: $100,000 to $5,000,000+

Credit Assistance Services

Our specific credit assistance services include:

  • Credit Product Selection: Suggesting appropriate credit products and lenders based on your needs
  • Application Assistance: Helping prepare and submit credit applications
  • Document Preparation: Assisting with required documentation and compliance
  • Lender Negotiation: Negotiating terms and conditions on your behalf
  • Ongoing Support: Post-settlement assistance and account management
  • Refinancing Advice: Periodic review and refinancing opportunities

Important Note: Commercial Focus

Our services are specifically designed for commercial finance and business lending. We do not provide consumer credit assistance for personal purchases unrelated to business activities.

How We Are Remunerated

Commission-Based Model

We operate on a commission-based model where we receive payments from lenders when successful finance arrangements are established. This means our services are typically provided at no direct cost to you.

Upfront Commission

We receive an upfront commission from the lender when your loan settles. This commission is calculated as a percentage of the loan amount:

  • Equipment Finance: 0.5% to 2.5% of loan amount
  • Commercial Property: 0.6% to 1.2% of loan amount
  • Working Capital: 1.0% to 3.0% of facility limit
  • Personal Lending: 0.65% to 1.1% of loan amount

Commission rates vary based on loan type, lender, loan amount, and terms. Specific commission amounts will be disclosed in your quote.

Trail Commission

For some loan products, we may receive ongoing trail commission throughout the life of the loan:

  • Home Loans: 0.15% to 0.25% per annum of outstanding balance
  • Some Commercial Facilities: 0.10% to 0.50% per annum
  • Line of Credit Facilities: 0.25% to 0.75% per annum of limit

Trail commission is paid by the lender and does not affect your interest rate or loan terms.

Direct Fees (When Applicable)

In specific circumstances, we may charge direct fees for our services:

  • Complex Structuring Fee: $2,500 to $15,000 for sophisticated commercial structures
  • Due Diligence Fee: $1,500 to $5,000 for detailed financial analysis
  • Documentation Review Fee: $500 to $2,000 for complex legal documentation
  • Unsuccessful Application Fee: $500 to $2,500 (only if disclosed upfront)

Important: Any direct fees will be clearly disclosed and agreed upon in writing before we commence work on your application.

Third-Party Payments

We may receive payments or benefits from third parties including:

  • Lender marketing and training events
  • Professional development and education programs
  • Technology platform and software subsidies
  • Industry conference and networking event support

These benefits do not influence our credit assistance services or product recommendations, which are based on your specific needs and circumstances.

Transparency Commitment

We are committed to full transparency about our remuneration. Before proceeding with any application, we will provide you with specific details of:

  • Exact commission amounts we will receive
  • Any direct fees that may apply
  • Alternative remuneration structures available
  • How our remuneration may influence our recommendations

Conflicts of Interest

Managing Conflicts of Interest

We are committed to managing conflicts of interest to ensure our recommendations are in your best interests. We have policies and procedures to identify, manage, and disclose any potential conflicts.

Remuneration-Based Conflicts

Different lenders may pay different commission rates, which could potentially influence our recommendations. To manage this conflict:

  • We maintain a diverse panel of lenders with competitive rates
  • Our recommendations are based on a comparison process considering your needs
  • We disclose commission rates before you sign any applications
  • We provide explanations for our recommendations beyond just commission

Lender Relationships

We maintain ongoing business relationships with multiple lenders. These relationships:

  • Enable us to access competitive products and faster processing
  • May include volume-based incentives that we disclose transparently
  • Do not prevent us from recommending the most suitable lender for your needs
  • Are managed through our conflicts of interest policy

Related Entity Interests

We do not have ownership interests in any lending institutions. However:

  • We may have referral arrangements with professional service providers
  • Any financial interests in related businesses are disclosed
  • We maintain independence in our credit assistance services
  • Referrals to related entities are disclosed and optional

Conflict Management Strategies

When conflicts of interest arise, we manage them by:

  • Disclosure: Full disclosure of any relevant conflicts before providing advice
  • Avoidance: Declining to act where conflicts cannot be managed appropriately
  • Management: Implementing procedures to ensure conflicts don't compromise our advice
  • Documentation: Recording conflicts and management strategies in your file

Your Right to Know

You have the right to ask about any potential conflicts of interest that may affect our services. We encourage you to discuss any concerns about our recommendations or the basis for our advice.

Complaints and Dispute Resolution

Internal Complaints Process

If you have a complaint about our credit services, we have an internal dispute resolution process to address your concerns promptly and fairly.

1

Lodge Your Complaint

Contact us with your complaint using any of the following methods:

  • Phone: 1300 XXX XXX
  • Email: complaints@edgeviewfinance.com.au
  • Written: Post to our registered office
  • In Person: During business hours

Please provide details of your complaint including relevant dates, documents, and your desired outcome.

2

Acknowledgment

We will acknowledge your complaint within 1 business day and provide:

  • Confirmation we have received your complaint
  • A reference number for tracking
  • The name of the person handling your complaint
  • Information about our complaints process
3

Investigation

We will investigate your complaint by:

  • Reviewing all relevant documentation and records
  • Consulting with relevant staff and systems
  • Seeking additional information if required
  • Considering applicable laws and industry standards

Timeframe: Most complaints are resolved within 21 days. Complex matters may take up to 45 days.

4

Resolution

We will provide a written response including:

  • Our findings and the reasons for our decision
  • Any remedial action we will take
  • Any compensation or corrective measures
  • Information about external dispute resolution options

External Dispute Resolution

If you are not satisfied with our internal resolution, or if we do not respond within 45 days, you can lodge a complaint with the Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA).

Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA)

Website: www.afca.org.au

Phone: 1800 931 678

Email: info@afca.org.au

Post: Australian Financial Complaints Authority
GPO Box 3, Melbourne VIC 3001

AFCA is a free service established by the Australian Government to resolve complaints about financial services. AFCA can make determinations that are binding on us, but not on you.

Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC)

Website: www.asic.gov.au

Phone: 1300 300 630

Online: ASIC Report Misconduct page

ASIC regulates our credit licence and can investigate breaches of credit legislation and licensing conditions.

No Cost Complaints Service

Our internal complaints handling service is provided at no cost to you. AFCA's service is also free. You may wish to seek independent legal advice, though this is at your own cost.

Privacy and Personal Information

How We Handle Your Information

We collect, use, and disclose personal information (including credit information) in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988 and the Credit Reporting Privacy Code 2014.

Information Collection

We collect personal information including:

  • Identity and contact information
  • Financial and employment details
  • Credit information from credit reporting bodies
  • Information from referees and other sources

Information Use

We use your information to:

  • Assess your credit needs and borrowing capacity
  • Recommend suitable credit products
  • Submit applications to lenders
  • Manage our ongoing relationship with you

Information Disclosure

We may disclose your information to:

  • Credit providers and their representatives
  • Credit reporting bodies
  • Professional service providers
  • Our related entities and service providers

Your Privacy Rights

You have the right to:

  • Access your personal information
  • Request correction of inaccurate information
  • Make a privacy complaint
  • Opt out of direct marketing

Full Privacy Policy

For complete information about how we handle your personal information, please read our full Privacy Policy. You can also contact our Privacy Officer at privacy@edgeviewfinance.com.au with any privacy-related questions.

Important Legal Information

General Disclaimer

This Credit Guide contains general information only and does not consider your specific needs, objectives, or financial situation. Before making any decisions about credit products, you should consider whether they are appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent financial advice if necessary.

Credit Product Information

Credit products have different features, costs, and risks. Interest rates, fees, and terms vary between lenders and may change. You should read and understand all product disclosure statements and terms and conditions before proceeding with any credit application.

Responsible Lending Obligations

We are required to comply with responsible lending obligations under the National Consumer Credit Protection Act 2009. This includes making reasonable inquiries about your requirements and objectives, taking reasonable steps to verify your financial situation, and making reasonable assessments about whether credit products are unsuitable for you.

Limitation of Liability

While we take reasonable care in providing credit assistance services, we cannot guarantee that credit applications will be approved or that specific terms will be achieved. Our liability is limited to the re-performance of our services or refund of fees paid (where applicable).
